scrubGroups.name: The Strait
scrubGroups.settingTitle: She crosses the dark strait, every night.
storyTitles: the galley warm, nothing to carry
the wind freshening off the headland
a hard sea between the cliffs
the worst past, the deck put right
through the narrows, and home by dawn
scrubGroups.throughline: the narrows — the pressure you pass through to get free
env: the cliffs hold the wind off; the channel flat, both shore-lights burning
the wind funnels off the headland; the channel chops up
the cliffs loom close, the sea heaped and roaring between them
the cliffs fall back; the wind sighs and eases, the deck put right
dawn on open water, the tight channel astern
scrubGroups.buildingBlock: a struggle of endurance against a known, external pressure — the Pilot's lens
scrubGroups.audienceServe: Addresses anxiety by framing external pressure as a temporary, survivable channel.
scrubGroups.wbRead: Implies stories of endurance, with a cast focused on mutual support through constriction.

scrubGroups.name: The Night Crossing
scrubGroups.settingTitle: Every night, the same dark crossing.
storyTitles: the far shore slow to come
the wind up, and held steady
the squall behind, the kettle on
the far shore close, and first light
scrubGroups.throughline: the far shore — a promise lost in the storm, found at dawn
env: the far shore a low string of lights, a patient promise
rain hazes the far lights; it stops getting nearer
no shore, no horizon — only the rain and the compass glow
the far lights swim up again, closer than you dared hope
the harbour close, a lone bell, first light on the landing
scrubGroups.buildingBlock: a struggle of faith when the destination vanishes — the Manifest-Keeper's lens
scrubGroups.audienceServe: Addresses low self-worth by showing that hope remains even when the goal is unseen.
scrubGroups.wbRead: Implies stories of faith, with a cast focused on holding hope when the destination is lost.

scrubGroups.name: The Dark Water
scrubGroups.settingTitle: The dark water carries you over.
storyTitles: the deep heaving, and she holds
the sea settling, the worst gone
the water still, the dawn on it
scrubGroups.throughline: the deep — the indifferent power that carries you anyway
env: glass-black water holding the moon's road
a long, deep swell begins to lift the hull
the boat sinks in the troughs between immense dark waves
the swell subsides, the great tension letting go
dawn on the calm that held you all along
scrubGroups.buildingBlock: a struggle of acceptance against an overwhelming force — the Deckhand's lens
scrubGroups.audienceServe: Addresses ADHD patterns by reframing the overwhelming world as a force that carries, not crushes.
scrubGroups.wbRead: Implies stories of acceptance, with a cast focused on letting go of control within chaos.

subrange.label: cozy
subrange.text: below in the galley, the kettle on — the deep glass-black and quiet past the porthole

subrange.label: warm
subrange.text: on the warm side of the wheelhouse, watching the dark water slide past

subrange.label: intense
subrange.text: alone at the stern, just you and the black water, the lamps small behind you

subrange.label: cozy
subrange.text: the cook bracing the pot, your cup rocking; nothing asked of you yet

subrange.label: warm
subrange.text: at the rail with the Deckhand, feeling the swell come up under you

subrange.label: intense
subrange.text: on deck as the first long swell lifts the hull — your hands already gripping the rail

subrange.label: cozy
subrange.text: the four of you wedged in the galley, tea and the worst of it shut outside

subrange.label: warm
subrange.text: in the wheelhouse doorway, half in, half out, watching the Pilot hold the wheel

subrange.label: intense
subrange.text: out in it, the deck heaving — the Deckhand's hand on your shoulder: “stop steering. let her carry you.”

subrange.label: cozy
subrange.text: the worst gone — dry socks, the manifest-keeper logging the night, quiet

subrange.label: warm
subrange.text: on deck as it eases, the Deckhand coiling line beside you, not saying much

subrange.label: intense
subrange.text: still white-knuckled long after you needed to be — learning, slowly, to unclench

subrange.label: cozy
subrange.text: dawn in the galley, the fifth bowl set, the deep flat and bright

subrange.label: warm
subrange.text: at the rail as the light comes, the far shore a line, the crew quiet around you

subrange.label: intense
subrange.text: alone at the bow as the light comes — letting the held thing go, at last, over the side

ambients.name: The Deep
ambients.why: Cold + dark + low-saturation: the sea is vast and doesn't care — and that's SAFE here, not oppressive. One pale gleam (the moon's road) is the carry: something holds you even when it's too much. Best fit for «surrender as support».

ambients.name: Iyashikei Mist
ambients.why: Softer, warmer-grey, hushed — Mushishi-calm. Strongest on the cozy floor (the safest read), but the deep feels less vast / less indifferent.

ambients.name: Carried to Dawn
ambients.why: The cold deep that resolves into a low dawn-gold accent — it carries the arc's 'come home to first light', but two temperatures make it busier than a single cold key.

characters.name: the Cook
characters.is: the galley meal that happens anyway, imperfect — homely warmth

characters.name: the Pilot
characters.is: the held heading through the dark — the steady cool

characters.name: the Manifest-keeper
characters.is: your carry, logged at full weight — quiet, precise

characters.name: the Deckhand
characters.is: the warm hand on your shoulder — “let her carry you”

mood.eli5: FEW colors when it's cozy (one feeling: safe — the deep + the galley warmth); MORE as it turns intense (the storm: the whole crew at once). The number of colors IS how much is happening. The cold deep is the sea that doesn't care; the warm colors are the people — because the warmth that lets you let go is a person's hand, not the water.

ambients.note: Its deep, indifferent cool directly serves the core metaphor, but the darkness needs careful handling in layout to avoid feeling heavy.
The safest floor for the audience, perfectly hitting the iyashikei brief, but it weakens the core 'Dark Water' metaphor of surrender.
Beautifully encapsulates the story's resolution, but having that hope baked into the base ambient may flatten the emotional journey.

vet.characterHarmony: The cool-toned crew harmonise as part of the steady ambient, allowing the warm-toned Cook and Deckhand to emerge as clear, supportive focal points.
fixes: Manifest-keeper #5b817c vs base contrast (4.16) is low for text; recommend lightening to meet 4.5:1.
vet.oneLine: The vast, indifferent cool is the safe foundation; human warmth is the specific, focused support that emerges from it.

characters: the Deckhand — weathered amber jacket, sun-browned hands, a cold cyan rim-light off the deep (the one warm figure)
the Pilot — muted slate-blue oils, cool and steady, face half in shadow
the Cook — soft terracotta apron, galley-lamp glow on her, warm but low-saturation
the Manifest-keeper — deep teal-grey coat, ink-dark, a pale-silver ledger
objects: the lamp — a small warm-brass pool against the cold dark
the rail — wet near-black iron with a cold cyan sheen
the kettle / galley — soft terracotta warmth, steam catching the lamp
the manifest / log — deep teal-grey leather, pale-silver pages
trophy: a cold pewter-silver form catching the moon's cyan gleam on the deep-indigo dark — luminous, never gold
items: an amber coil of mooring line — each a low-saturation note against the deep
achievementIcons: flat icon on the deep-indigo base · a pale-silver line · the cold-cyan accent for the earned glow · one warm amber note when the achievement is about being carried

rules: NEVER name the colour — render it through the objects + a sensation (Chekhov: the glint, not “blue”)
PAIR every colour beat with a non-visual sense — cold iron under the hand, salt and diesel, the kettle's tick (sight alone doesn't immerse)
DICTION: the exact word carries the tone — “slate” sky reads colder than “silver”; cool = still/deep/far, warm = close/lit
ONE mood paragraph — blend the objects, never an inventory list
IYASHIKEI: foreground texture — the rustle, the warm cup, the engine's hum — over plot
prose.example: Below, the galley holds its amber pool of lamp-light against the cold dark past the porthole. The kettle ticks; steam beads the glass. Out there the deep slides by, slate and still, and asks nothing of you.

perTone.note: Perfectly establishes a baseline of sanctuary and care, insulating the player from the overwhelm.
Excellent bridge tone, using observation of competence to model trust and safety from a slight distance.
Masterfully handles intensity as embodied presence, directly teaching the core metaphor without violating the safety floor.

subrangeAudit.oneLine: This set brilliantly maps emotional intensity to physical space—from the sheltered galley to the exposed deck—offering a clear and safe spectrum of engagement with the core metaphor.

expertPanel.audience: anxiety (the braced, hypervigilant body) · low self-worth (care must be earned; I'm a burden) · ADHD/overwhelm (no spare working memory; too many open loops)

experts.name: Mara Oduya
experts.mindset: The anxious body is BRACED — hypervigilant, scanning for the next thing to lunge at it, never fully exhaling. They read a room's temperature before its content. They don't need stimulation; they need a signal it's safe to unclench.
experts.colours: Desaturated, low-contrast — held-deep blue-greens that say 'nothing here moves suddenly.' NO alarm-red, no hard high-contrast edges (the eye reads them as threat). ONE low-saturation warm point (the galley lamp) = a hearth, never a spotlight. The dark is soft, not a void.
experts.cast: A presence that is CALM FIRST — the Cook's steadiness is contagious; an overwhelmed nervous system co-regulates off a settled other. The calm is the medicine, before any words.
experts.donts: Don't perform urgency or ambush. The intense tone must be WEIGHT (a held depth), never a jump-scare or a rising threat.

experts.name: Devin Sato
experts.mindset: The overwhelmed mind has too many open loops and zero spare working memory. Every extra choice, colour, or character on screen is a tax. It bounces the instant the first screen asks it to MANAGE anything. «Surrender as support» is exactly right — the world should do the carrying so the player manages nothing.
experts.colours: LOW count at cozy — two, maybe three. The palette GROWS only as the player chooses to go deeper (depth is opt-in, never dumped). No flashing, no two focal points competing for the eye.
experts.cast: ONE focal character per tone — a single warm face, never a crowd at the floor. The crew can exist around the edge, but the frame holds one (Cook → Pilot/Keeper → Deckhand, as the player descends).
experts.donts: Don't front-load the cast. Don't make the player track state to enjoy the cozy floor. Never require a decision to receive comfort.

experts.name: Lena Hart
experts.role: GENRE — iyashikei creator (cozy games for the burnt-out)
experts.mindset: This audience does NOT want to be fixed — being fixed implies they're broken, which is the wound. They want to be ACCOMPANIED. The fantasy is: someone competent is near, the night is bounded, and nothing is required of me.
experts.colours: The texture of comfort is multisensory-implied — colours that read as warm light on worn wood, steam off the pot, the blanket-weight of the dark. The palette should feel TOUCHED, not designed.
experts.cast: Quiet competence that WITNESSES, never coaches-at. The intense tone = a DEEPER witness (they see you at your lowest and simply stay), not a louder or higher-stakes one. Escalate by deepening presence, never by raising stakes.
experts.donts: No character diagnoses or 'improves' the player. Don't escalate the intense tone with plot peril — the strait is weather, never an enemy.

experts.name: Sam Reyes
experts.mindset: The core belief is 'I'm a burden; care must be earned; if they really knew me they'd leave.' They flinch from warmth that feels conditional or performed, and they quietly TEST whether the care survives their worst night.
experts.colours: No saccharine warmth — the bright pink/gold that performs care reads as fake to them. The warmth must be LOW and TRUE (a steady amber). The deep blue-green carries the whole metaphor: the water holds you whether or not you deserve it.
experts.cast: A cast that gives care UNCONDITIONALLY — «the indifferent deep that carries you anyway» IS the medicine: you don't have to hold yourself up, and nothing is asked back. The Manifest-keeper's lane (your carry logged at full weight, same ink as everyone's) is the self-worth dose.
experts.donts: Care must never be contingent on the player 'doing well.' No praising performance. The intense tone must never feel like a test the player can fail.

experts.name: Haruki Sone
experts.role: GENRE — iyashikei critic (the canon: Midnight Diner · Mushishi · ARIA · Spiritfarer)
experts.mindset: I know what makes healing slice-of-life LAND: the keeper is a near-SILENT witness (Midnight Diner), not a mentor; the warmth lives in the GOODBYE and the small gesture, never the systems (Spiritfarer's lesson); a tranquil frame can carry real darkness without breaking (Mushishi); and 'nothing happens' is the FEATURE, not a defect (ARIA). The nightly RITUAL is the warmth engine.
experts.colours: the canon's palette is restrained + lived-in — lamplight on worn wood, the dark water held at a calm distance; never the slick, never the saccharine.
experts.cast: the crew WITNESS each rider and let them go — the episodic per-night vignette over a quiet throughline; the keeper logs your carry 'at full weight' and releases it.
experts.donts: no Spiritfarer grind/chores gating the warmth · the dark strait is ATMOSPHERIC, never a threat mechanic · don't let the perpetual RETURN dilute the goodbye payoff.

framework.palette: Desaturated held-deep blue-greens (the carrying water) · ONE low-saturation warm hearth-point (an earned, true amber — never saccharine) · the palette GROWS only with chosen depth (ADHD opt-in load) · never alarm-red, never hard high-contrast edges, never performed-warm pink.
framework.cast: Quiet competence that WITNESSES unconditionally (the self-worth dose) · ONE calm-first focal per tone, co-regulating (anxiety) · the crew HOLDS across all three tones (cohesion) — Cook → Pilot/Keeper → Deckhand as the player descends.
framework.tones: cozy = buffered safety (low load, calm presence) · warm = trusted witness (the care survives being known) · intense = carried at your lowest, SAFELY (a deeper witness — WEIGHT, never threat, never a test you can fail).
framework.floor: Every expert agrees: the intense tone deepens PRESENCE, it never raises THREAT. Safe at every tone, for every audience thread.

perExpert.name: Mara Oduya
perExpert.note: Her somatic focus on anxiety is precise and directly translates into actionable visual and character design principles.

perExpert.name: Devin Sato
perExpert.note: His focus on cognitive load is critical, providing clear, restrictive rules that protect the player's limited working memory.

perExpert.name: Lena Hart
perExpert.note: Her 'accompany, don't fix' insight is a foundational genre principle that also serves as a core safety rule for the audience.

perExpert.name: Sam Reyes
perExpert.note: His framing of unconditional care as an indifferent, environmental force is a powerful and safe metaphor for this audience.

perExpert.name: Haruki Sone
perExpert.note: His canon-based analysis provides robust genre guardrails, specifically rejecting mechanics that would harm the audience experience.

vet.oneLine: The panel's synthesis is a masterclass in designing for a vulnerable audience, earning the right to brief the project.

sets.name: The Warm Hand
sets.deepening: the Deckhand's hand on your shoulder — “let her carry you” — surrender as being physically HELD.

sets.name: The Unlit Middle
sets.deepening: the Pilot holds the heading while you stop steering — surrender as letting someone KEEP THE COURSE when you can't see.

sets.name: Logged at Full Weight
sets.deepening: the Manifest-keeper logs your carry at full weight at your lowest — surrender as letting your WORTH BE RECORDED when you can't hold it yourself.

perSet.note: The most embodied and direct expression of «Surrender as support».
A powerful metaphor for executive dysfunction, but less emotionally cohesive.
A subtle, profound take that deeply resonates with the iyashikei lens.

castAudition.why: It offers the most cohesive, embodied progression of care that directly addresses the audience's physical anxiety.
castAudition.runnerUpGem: Its concept of unconditional validation—being 'logged at full weight'—is a profound gem for the low self-worth audience.

question.title: Audition a protagonist/rival who MIRRORS the audience's own problem
question.body: Right now the SUBRANGE makeup auditions the CREW — the coaches/keepers who WITNESS the player. The owner's proposal: ALSO audition, per subrange, a PROTAGONIST/RIVAL character who embodies the AUDIENCE'S OWN PROBLEM — a MIRROR of the player themselves (a fellow passenger who carries the same struggle: the braced/overwhelmed/burden-believing state, who can't let the deep carry them). The player would WITNESS this mirror — and witness them being carried/cared-for anyway — as a way of seeing their own struggle from the outside, with compassion. The metaphor «surrender as support» would be enacted on the mirror, for the player to receive vicariously.

perExpert.name: Mara Oduya
perExpert.why: Watching another braced body could be profoundly activating, putting my own system on high alert instead of co-regulating down. If their struggle is too raw, it's a threat signal, not a soothing one.
perExpert.risk: Somatic contagion — the player's nervous system mirrors the mirror's distress, leading to *more* bracing, not less.
perExpert.safe_how: The mirror's state must be presented as *fatigue* from bracing, not active panic. Their struggle should be quiet and internal, inviting gentle witness rather than alarm.

perExpert.name: Devin Sato
perExpert.why: You're adding another character for my brain to track, another open loop. Even if I'm just witnessing, it's a cognitive load I don't have the spoons for; my focus will splinter and I'll just feel more overwhelmed.
perExpert.risk: Cognitive load. The mirror character becomes one more demand on the player's exhausted working memory, turning a refuge into a task.
perExpert.safe_how: The 'mirror' cannot be a separate character to track. Their story must be woven into the environment or the crew's incidental dialogue, as ambient texture rather than a focal point.

perExpert.name: Lena Hart
perExpert.why: The word 'rival' is an immediate red flag in an iyashikei world; there is no competition, only co-existence. Framing them as a 'problem' re-introduces the 'fixing' dynamic we're trying to escape.
perExpert.risk: Pathologizing the experience. By labeling the mirror's state a 'problem,' the game implies the player is also a 'problem,' reinforcing the shame of being burnt-out.
perExpert.safe_how: The 'rival' must be reframed as a 'fellow traveler.' They aren't a problem to solve, but simply another person on the same journey, sharing the same quiet space.

perExpert.name: Sam Reyes
perExpert.why: Seeing someone *exactly like me*—difficult, withdrawn, convinced they're a burden—receive unconditional care without having to perform for it can bypass my own defenses. It models a possibility my core belief says can't exist.
perExpert.risk: Comparison and self-invalidation. The player might think, 'Of course *they* get care, they're more worthy/less broken than I am,' if the mirror isn't calibrated perfectly.
perExpert.safe_how: The care shown to the mirror must be incredibly subtle, non-performative, and persistent even when the mirror tries to refuse it. It must feel like quiet, sturdy presence, not a grand gesture.

consults.verdict: on the mark WITH a reframe
consults.reframe: The 'rival' must become a 'fellow traveler' — an ambient, non-demanding presence who shares the space, not a focal point to be managed.
consults.perTone: Yes; in cozy tones they are simply tired, in intense tones their struggle is more visible, but the crew's quiet, unconditional care remains the constant.
consults.oneLine: A mirror works only if they're not a task to manage or a problem to fix, but quiet proof that even on the worst nights, you're allowed to just be.

mirror.premise: the audience's own problem, witnessed in another PASSENGER — «surrender as support» enacted on them (you watch someone be carried), received vicariously (not a rival, not the protagonist)

conditions.label: ambient, not tracked — woven into the world + the crew's incidental care; no arc, zero follow-up, never a focal to manage
fatigue, not panic — quiet, internal bracing-tiredness; invites gentle witness, never alarm or active distress
mundane, persistent care — the crew's care for them is unremarkable + non-performative + persists even if refused; never a special intervention (earned care = the wound)
a fellow traveler, not a rival/problem — shares the quiet space; never an adversary, never a thing to be fixed

perTone.text: across the galley, another passenger sits over a mug they haven’t touched — just tired, the way you’re tired. the Cook sets a second bowl by them without a word, same as yours.
at the far rail the same passenger grips the wood too hard, bracing for a swell that isn’t that big. the Pilot drifts over and stands the watch beside them — says nothing, just stays.
out in the worst of it they’ve stopped trying to hold themselves up. the manifest-keeper logs them in — same ink, full weight — and doesn’t look up to see if they’ve earned it.

perCondition.note: The vignettes are self-contained and observational, successfully avoiding any sense of a quest or tracked state.
The language focuses on weariness and internal bracing, not active, alarming distress.
The actions are normalized and non-performative, reinforcing that care is a default state, not an intervention.
The framing maintains a respectful distance, allowing for vicarious experience without creating a social obligation.

perTone.note: The 'same as yours' detail is a masterful touch, immediately establishing the mirror and the mundane nature of the care.
The Pilot's silent companionship is a strong embodiment of support without intrusion, fitting the warm register perfectly.
This lands the core metaphor of 'surrender as support' with powerful, restrained language that deepens presence without raising threat.

vet.oneLine: Approved; the mirror is a masterclass in quiet resonance, landing the core metaphor safely across all tones.

cozy.coachVoice: The Cook's voice is one of quiet, physical presence. They witness through action—the steadying of a pot, the silent offering of a bowl. Their language is gesture, communicating safety and permission to rest without the burden of words.
beats: The Cook ladles steaming broth into two bowls. One is set before you. The other is placed by the other passenger, who just nods. No words are needed.
As the ship pitches, the Cook takes a thick, woollen blanket from a sea-locker and drapes it over your shoulders before turning to brace a rattling cabinet.
After the storm, the Cook methodically wipes down the steel counter. They pause, looking out the porthole at the settling sea, a shared moment of quiet.
cozy.narrowedBy: The convergence of the iyashikei genre's value on restraint ('ma') and the audience's state of overwhelm pre-decided that care must be expressed through silent, non-demanding gestures rather than words.

perExpert.name: Mara Oduya
perExpert.note: The Cook's steady, physical presence perfectly embodies the 'calm first' principle of co-regulation.

perExpert.name: Devin Sato
perExpert.note: The content correctly establishes a single, warm focal character, requiring no decisions or state-tracking for comfort.

perExpert.name: Lena Hart
perExpert.note: This is a prime example of witnessing through quiet competence; care is shown through action, not coaching.

perExpert.name: Sam Reyes
perExpert.note: Care is delivered unconditionally through gestures like the blanket and broth, asking nothing in return.

perExpert.name: Haruki Sone
perExpert.note: The beats feel like self-contained vignettes, using the atmospheric strait as a backdrop for warmth, not a threat.

vet.oneLine: The writer perfectly captured the brief's 'calm first' principle through quiet, unconditional, and witnessing action.

supporting.presence: Glimpsed for a moment through the galley porthole, their back to us, methodically polishing a brass fitting under a soft deck light.
A large, ginger cat is curled asleep on a worn chair in the corner, occasionally stirring with a soft, breathy sigh.

cozy.note: They are ambient details of competence and safety, enriching the sanctuary of the galley without introducing characters to track.

audit.note: The world coheres through a consistent vocabulary of deep sea blues, misty greys, and warm brass/dawn accents, shifting in combination to reflect the time and tone.

premises.premise: The quiet manifest-keeper invites you to find a name on her list, a fellow passenger's. This small act of witness prompts a reflection on your own reasons for this journey.
The Deckhand, securing lines, wordlessly offers you one to hold. It isn't a request for help, but an invitation to feel the sea's deep rhythm as the first swells arrive.
The Pilot gestures for you to stand in the wheelhouse doorway. From this place of protected witness, you watch the immense, indifferent waves, feeling the ship endure.
The Cook wordlessly hands you a heavy, warm bowl. The simple, grounding act of holding it helps you release the storm's lingering tension from your body.
As dawn breaks, you find a small, carved bird left by an unseen passenger. It’s a silent acknowledgement of the shared passage as the far shore appears on the horizon.

range.variety_add: A moment of observing non-human life, like bioluminescent plankton or a distant whale call, indifferent to the storm, reinforcing the deep's living presence.
range.note: The range provides distinct emotional and interactive hooks for each phase of the arc, focusing on different modes of quiet interaction.

rangeReview.flag: Success depends on the writer's ability to convey weight through non-verbal cues and the unsaid, honoring the 'ma' (meaningful pause).

expertsGate.name: Lena Hart
expertsGate.role: GENRE — iyashikei creator (cozy games for the burnt-out)

expertsGate.name: Haruki Sone
expertsGate.role: GENRE — iyashikei critic (the canon: Midnight Diner · Mushishi · ARIA · Spiritfarer)

calm-water.castPick: The Manifest-keeper — their quiet, precise nature is a perfect mirror for the glass-still water, turning the external scene into a prompt for internal accounting.
calm-water.premiseHoned: Watching the perfect, unbroken reflection of the moon on the water, the Manifest-keeper takes a quiet moment to log the weight they carry inside, observing it with the same dispassionate care as the ship's cargo.
calm-water.review: Establishes a strong, quiet baseline for the character's internal journey.

castAudition.name: The Manifest-keeper
castAudition.note: their quiet, observational precision perfectly mirrors the water's stillness

castAudition.name: The Cook
castAudition.note: their gentle presence offers a background warmth but lacks the scene's core stillness

castAudition.name: The Pilot
castAudition.note: their steady competence is reassuring but less emotionally present than a shared quiet

castAudition.name: The Deckhand
castAudition.note: their active comfort feels intrusive in a moment of pure tranquility

calm-water.whyWon: The Manifest-keeper's non-intrusive, witnessing presence best embodies the scene's low-arousal calm and the 'nothing happens, beautifully' iyashikei principle.

candidates.name: Brass & Twilight
candidates.note: Establishes a strong, classic night-ferry feel with a warm, grounding accent.

candidates.name: Verdigris & Stillness
candidates.note: A unique, contemplative palette that leans into the quiet, cool aspect of the sea.

candidates.name: Faded Indigo
candidates.note: A balanced, gentle introduction that feels safe and slightly melancholic.

paletteAudition.whyWon: The brass accent provides a strong, tangible point of warmth that grounds the world and the 'cozy night-ferry' genre promise immediately.
paletteAudition.runnerUpGem: Its unique verdigris/seafoam identity perfectly captures the restrained, low-arousal mood of the iyashikei lens.

rising-wind.castPick: The Pilot — their steady, cool competence is the focus, reassuring the player not with words but with small, expert actions that meet the rising sea.
rising-wind.premiseHoned: From the wheelhouse doorway, the player witnesses the Pilot's hands on the wheel, making minute, calm adjustments that harmonize with the sea's growing swell, embodying a competence that soothes rather than excites.
rising-wind.review: A powerful, non-verbal scene that builds trust in the world's safety through character.

castAudition.name: The Pilot
castAudition.note: their cool competence provides a direct, functional anchor against the rising uncertainty

castAudition.name: The Cook
castAudition.note: their continued ritual of preparing a meal offers a powerful, indirect form of stability

castAudition.name: The Deckhand
castAudition.note: their offer of direct comfort is a valid response but could feel premature

castAudition.name: The Manifest-keeper
castAudition.note: their quiet focus feels disconnected and too passive for the rising external energy

rising-wind.whyWon: As the environment becomes uncertain, the Pilot's steady hand on the wheel is the most direct and grounding reassurance against overwhelm.

candidates.name: Sea Salt & Spray
candidates.note: A very literal interpretation of the weather, focusing on cool, atmospheric greys and the feeling of spray.

candidates.name: Iron & Overcast
candidates.note: Communicates a sense of weight and substance, as if the air itself is getting heavier.

candidates.name: Deep Moss & Rain
candidates.note: An organic, earthy take on the building storm, connecting the sea to something more ancient.

paletteAudition.whyWon: The iron grey-blue provides the best tonal transition from 'calm water', adding weight and seriousness without losing the core tranquility.
paletteAudition.runnerUpGem: The deep moss green is a beautifully unexpected and characterful choice that feels very grounded and natural.

the-storm.castPick: The Deckhand — their role as a grounding, warm presence is essential here, transforming a potentially terrifying moment into an experience of being held and supported.
the-storm.premiseHoned: As the boat heaves in the dark troughs, the Deckhand invites the player to a simple, grounding task below deck—methodically polishing a brass compass—the rhythmic motion and their quiet presence an anchor in the chaos.
the-storm.review: Transforms a potential high-arousal threat into a core moment of somatic grounding.

castAudition.name: The Deckhand
castAudition.note: their direct, somatic comfort is the perfect antidote to the scene's peak overwhelm

castAudition.name: The Pilot
castAudition.note: their unseen competence is the foundation of safety, but it's too remote for immediate comfort

castAudition.name: The Cook
castAudition.note: their persistence is a symbol of hope, but less immediate than physical reassurance

castAudition.name: The Manifest-keeper
castAudition.note: their quiet, precise nature is completely invalidated by the scene's raw chaos

the-storm.whyWon: The Deckhand's offer of direct, physical comfort ('a warm hand on your shoulder') meets the player's acute need for a human anchor in a moment of crisis.

candidates.name: Void & Instrument Glow
candidates.note: A clean, modern take on the storm's peak, emphasizing technology as a source of safety.

candidates.name: Ink & Phosphor
candidates.note: Invokes a sense of nostalgia and reliability with its retro-tech accent, reinforcing the 'support' metaphor.

candidates.name: Abyss & Amber
candidates.note: A warm, high-contrast choice that could feel like a comforting hearth or a warning light.

paletteAudition.whyWon: The phosphor green accent is the most unique and tonally aligned with the iyashikei/age-prior, suggesting old, dependable technology that supports without alarm.
paletteAudition.runnerUpGem: Its cool blue accent on near-black is incredibly effective at creating a sense of deep, focused calm.

settling-sea.castPick: The Cook — embodying homely warmth and restoration, their simple offer of shared work in the galley is the perfect ritual for marking the return to calm.
settling-sea.premiseHoned: In the warm galley, the Cook wordlessly hands the player a knife and a vegetable. The simple, shared labor of preparing a meal becomes a physical act of release, mirroring the easing of the sea.
settling-sea.review: Excellent use of a simple, communal activity to externalize an internal emotional shift.

castAudition.name: The Cook
castAudition.note: their offer of simple, imperfect nourishment perfectly matches the need for gentle recovery

castAudition.name: The Deckhand
castAudition.note: their continued presence is kind, but the need has shifted from crisis intervention to quiet care

castAudition.name: The Pilot
castAudition.note: their cool competence feels detached now that the crisis has passed and warmth is needed

castAudition.name: The Manifest-keeper
castAudition.note: their observational stance doesn't offer the restorative warmth the moment requires

settling-sea.whyWon: After the intensity of the storm, the Cook's homely ritual provides a tangible, restorative warmth that grounds the player in safety and care.

candidates.name: Bruised Sky & Silver
candidates.note: A poetic and emotionally resonant palette that captures the melancholy peace of a storm's aftermath.

candidates.name: Heather & Fog
candidates.note: A very soft, muted take that emphasizes comfort and the gentle dissipation of tension.

candidates.name: Worn Slate & Dew
candidates.note: A safe, clean palette that risks feeling too similar to the grey-blues of the 'rising wind' phase.

paletteAudition.whyWon: The 'bruised sky' violet is a distinct and evocative hue that perfectly captures the feeling of relief mixed with the memory of intensity.
paletteAudition.runnerUpGem: Its exceptional softness and muted heather tones are the purest expression of a low-arousal positive state.

first-light.castPick: The Manifest-keeper — having started as an observer of burdens, their final act as a celebrant of release brings their personal journey to a quiet, poignant close.
first-light.premiseHoned: At the stern, in the first light, the Manifest-keeper performs a small, precise ritual: taking a single stone from their pocket, representing a logged weight, and gently releasing it into the ship's wake.
first-light.review: Provides a poignant and satisfying character arc conclusion through a single, powerful gesture.

castAudition.name: The Manifest-keeper
castAudition.note: their role as a quiet witness perfectly frames the reflective weight of the journey's end

castAudition.name: The Pilot
castAudition.note: their steady presence signifies the journey's completion, a quiet shared accomplishment

castAudition.name: The Cook
castAudition.note: their warmth is a welcome backdrop, but less specific to the poignancy of arrival

castAudition.name: The Deckhand
castAudition.note: their direct comfort feels intrusive on a moment of personal, quiet reflection

first-light.whyWon: The Manifest-keeper's final, silent act of 'logging the carry' validates the passenger's entire journey, offering a poignant, non-verbal closure.

candidates.name: Rose & Ash
candidates.note: A gentle, emotional palette where the returning light feels tender and personal.

candidates.name: Tarnished Gold & Mist
candidates.note: A classic dawn palette, kept grounded and mature by the 'tarnished' quality of the gold.

candidates.name: Peach & Charcoal
candidates.note: A high-contrast option that makes the return of light feel significant and deeply warming against the lingering night.

paletteAudition.whyWon: The strong contrast between the soft peach and the dark charcoal base creates the most impactful and hopeful sense of 'renewal' to end the arc.
paletteAudition.runnerUpGem: Its gentle rose-on-ash combination is the most subtle and poetic, perfectly fitting the restrained emotional expression of the genre.

conflicts.issue: Both scenes use 'The Manifest-keeper', risking redundancy.
conflicts.resolution: The repetition is intentional, creating a narrative frame. Scene 1's role ('self-observer') is about acknowledging one's carried weight, while Scene 5's role ('celebrant') is about integrating the journey. The distinct roles and structures ('interior-noticing' vs. 'ritual-gesture') ensure the experiences are unique, providing thematic closure.

conflicts.issue: Both 'gentle-task' and 'shared-labor' involve simple actions and could feel similar.
conflicts.resolution: The contexts are oppositional. Scene 3's task is an internal, somatic anchor against overwhelm (survival). Scene 4's labor is external and collaborative, focused on restoration and re-engagement with another person (recovery).

coverage.note: This allocation provides a clear emotional arc with distinct player roles and interaction structures for each scene, using the repeated cast member to create a strong narrative frame.

objects: deep indigo sea under a charcoal sky
a ferry deck washed in slate-blue shadow
the dark, silent hull of the boat
characters: a figure wrapped in a night-blue coat
a face half-lit by old brass
items: a steaming mug the color of dark tea
a tarnished brass railing catching the lamplight

prose.rule: Describe the light by what it touches, not its source or color.

objects: a sea of churning slate grey
sky the color of gathering storm clouds
mist clinging to the air like pale smoke
characters: a silhouette against a gunmetal sky
items: a silver flask catching the dim light
a wool scarf the color of faded slate

prose.rule: Focus on the sound and feel of the wind, letting the visuals be secondary.

objects: a sea of pure, deep black
the absolute void of the night sky
characters: a steady form, their face traced by a faint green glow
eyes reflecting a distant, luminous signal
items: a compass dial with glowing green markings
a lantern casting a soft, verdant light

prose.rule: Treat the storm as a constant, rhythmic presence, like a heartbeat, not an event.

objects: a sea the color of deep twilight lavender
the deck damp and dark with settled rain
characters: a figure wrapped in a heavy, heather-grey blanket
a tired face in the pale, soft light
items: a ceramic cup of pale, steaming liquid
a silver locket, cool to the touch

prose.rule: Describe the absence of the storm's motion and sound, focusing on the new stillness.

objects: the horizon, a faint line of soft peach
charcoal-grey water catching the first blush of dawn
the dark silhouette of the approaching shore
characters: a face warmed by the new sun
a hand resting on the rail, bathed in a rosy glow
items: a worn book with a cover the color of faded rose

prose.rule: Show the light arriving by the way it changes the texture and color of the dark.

anxious.why: The arc coheres, but the storm peak with 'a hard sea between the cliffs' feels sharp and potentially unsafe, and the resolution is more practical than comforting.
The arc is perfectly cohesive and safe, with a storm that feels like a predictable weight and a resolution that brings deep comfort.
This arc feels cohesive and deeply safe because the storm's peak is explicitly paired with the reassurance that the vessel 'holds'.

low_worth.why: This world feels whole and its resolution lands perfectly, making the routine crossing feel like a meaningful, safe journey where just enduring is enough.
The world holds together around the feeling of quiet endurance, and the gentle, pressure-free resolution feels earned and deeply safe.
This world feels cohesive and elemental, and while the storm feels immense, the resolution is profound and the promise that 'she holds' makes it feel fundamentally safe.

adhd.why: This arc holds together perfectly as a coherent world, and the resolution feels earned and concrete with the deck being put right and the arrival home.
adhd.feeling: A steady hand at the helm

adhd.why: This world feels exceptionally coherent and directly addresses my needs with 'nothing asked of you', while its resolution with the kettle on is a perfect, cozy landing.
The arc is elemental and cohesive, creating a powerful sense of being held, and the resolution into stillness feels deeply renewing and safe.

iyashikei_fan.why: This arc feels like a complete, tangible journey through a specific place, and the resolution lands beautifully with the concrete action of putting the deck right.
This arc feels too gentle and lacks a convincing peak, making the world feel less dynamic and the resolution less earned.
The arc is perfectly balanced with a powerful but safe peak ('the deep heaving, and she holds'), creating a coherent and deeply satisfying journey that lands in a beautiful renewal.

thrill_seeker.why: This arc holds together as a coherent journey and resolves cleanly, but the peak of a 'hard sea' feels too routine and lacks the high stakes I crave.
While it coheres as a single world, the arc is incredibly flat; the 'storm' is just a steady wind, so the resolution lands with zero impact.
This arc coheres powerfully around the water itself and resolves well, offering a peak with a sense of immense weight, but the overall cozy frame is still not for me.

arc_complete.explanation: This arc is strong because its imagery is specific and active at every stage. The storm peak feels genuinely intense with the 'hard sea between the cliffs,' and the resolution feels earned, moving from active mending ('the deck put right') to a peaceful arrival.
arc_complete.opens: A story of repeated, skillful navigation through difficulty.
arc_complete.forecloses: A story where the storm is a random, surprising event.
arc_complete.seed:
▰▱▱▱▱   She crosses the dark strait, every night.  /  the galley warm, nothing to carry
▰▰▰▱▱   She crosses the dark strait, every night.  /  the wind freshening off the headland
▰▰▰▰▰   She crosses the dark strait, every night.  /  a hard sea between the cliffs
▰▰▱▱▱   She crosses the dark strait, every night.  /  the worst past, the deck put right
▰▱▱▱▱   She crosses the dark strait, every night.  /  through the narrows, and home by dawn

arc_complete.explanation: This arc fails to deliver an intense storm peak. The phrase 'the wind up, and held steady' suggests a manageable, sustained force rather than a chaotic, dangerous climax, which hollows out the core of the dynamic range.
arc_complete.opens: A story about endurance and routine.
arc_complete.forecloses: A story of a genuine, life-threatening crisis.
arc_complete.seed:
▰▱▱▱▱   Every night, the same dark crossing.  /  nothing asked of you tonight
▰▰▰▱▱   Every night, the same dark crossing.  /  the far shore slow to come
▰▰▰▰▰   Every night, the same dark crossing.  /  the wind up, and held steady
▰▰▱▱▱   Every night, the same dark crossing.  /  the squall behind, the kettle on
▰▱▱▱▱   Every night, the same dark crossing.  /  the far shore close, and first light

arc_complete.explanation: This arc has a powerful, cohesive feel, with all the imagery focused on the state of the water itself. The storm peak, 'the deep heaving, and she holds,' is both intense and personal, capturing a moment of profound struggle and endurance.
arc_complete.opens: A story about being subject to and surviving a powerful natural force.
arc_complete.forecloses: A story where human action is the primary driver of the conflict.
arc_complete.seed:
▰▱▱▱▱   The dark water carries you over.  /  calm, and the lamps lit
▰▰▰▱▱   The dark water carries you over.  /  a long swell building under
▰▰▰▰▰   The dark water carries you over.  /  the deep heaving, and she holds
▰▰▱▱▱   The dark water carries you over.  /  the sea settling, the worst gone
▰▱▱▱▱   The dark water carries you over.  /  the water still, the dawn on it

story_spawn.explanation: This arc provides a strong, classic narrative spine with a clear protagonist ('she') and a tangible external conflict. The specific, evocative language for each phase creates a solid foundation for a story about routine and resilience.
story_spawn.opens: A story of a recurring journey, personal fortitude, and navigating a known danger.
story_spawn.forecloses: A story where the conflict is primarily internal or abstract; this is grounded in a physical ordeal.

story_spawn.explanation: The arc feels passive, and its 'storm' phase, 'the wind up, and held steady,' lacks a sense of climax or overwhelming danger. This muted peak makes the overall dynamic range feel less compelling and harder to build a dramatic story upon.
story_spawn.opens: A meditative story about endurance, patience, and the psychological weight of a journey.
story_spawn.forecloses: A story of high-stakes action or a dramatic, chaotic confrontation with nature.

story_spawn.explanation: This arc treats the water itself as the primary character, creating a powerful, elemental conflict. The storm's peak, 'the deep heaving, and she holds,' is a potent and focused crisis point that strongly anchors a narrative about survival against an impersonal force.
story_spawn.opens: A mythic or symbolic tale of endurance against a vast, elemental power.
story_spawn.forecloses: A story focused on complex character choices or intricate plotting; this is about pure survival.

worldbuild.note: The richest metaphor reframes the nature of the struggle itself, not just the journey's beginning or end.

targetAge.lifeContext: burnout · overwhelm · low self-worth (a worn-out / limited-time state)
targetAge.register: cozy + warm core with an intense-as-DEPTH (poignancy) ceiling — calm tipping into weight, never thrill
targetAge.note: PROVISIONAL prior (playtest-calibrate, do not hard-code); the dialed tone overrides; content rating = All Ages

culture.name: East-Asian (iyashikei lineage)
culture.valorises: LOW-arousal positive states — calm, peacefulness, contentment (LAP)
culture.expression: indirect, restrained, the unsaid; harmony over assertion; the meaningful pause (ma)
culture.nudge: toward stillness + restraint + the implied — let silence and small gestures carry the weight

genre.name: cozy night-ferry iyashikei (healing slice-of-life)
genre.honour: the EPISODIC per-rider witness (board → witness → release, Midnight Diner) · the warmth in the GOODBYE / the small gesture, not the systems (Spiritfarer) · a tranquil frame can hold dark weight (Mushishi) · 'nothing happens, beautifully' is a FEATURE (ARIA) · the nightly RITUAL as a warmth engine
genre.traps: Spiritfarer's management/grind busywork (never gate warmth behind chores) · the dark strait as a contrast-THREAT (it's atmospheric, never a threat mechanic) · the perpetual RETURN diluting the goodbye payoff (the open bet)
